rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced ports and bindings.
AGREE annex blocks are syntactically defined as `annex agree {** ... **};` and should contain assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be scoped to the appropriate component type or implementation.
AGREE expressions must align with the actual data types of AADL ports and variables.
Do not include AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless explicitly typed in the model.
Boolean data range comparisons should only be used when the port is explicitly defined as an AGREE bool in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` for defining equations in AGREE with proper type alignment.
Ensure all visible identifiers in AGREE expressions are declared or available in the component scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and unit tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Implementation-level bindings and assignments in component type scopes
Model Analyst Agent's extraction of component structure
